domain: Xylem cohesion-tension theory (Dixon-Joly 1894): transpiration at leaves creates negative pressure transmitted down unbroken continuous water column via hydrogen-bond cohesion + xylem-wall adhesion + capillary action; supports water lift >100m; cavitation breaks column.
specific_mechanism: Top-down demand-side pull through passive continuous medium; tension transmitted via cohesion of identical-medium molecules; medium itself does no active work; small perforations prevent cavitation spread.
llm_application: XYLEM-TENSION continuous-cohesion residual propagation: augment standard residual stream with SECONDARY 'tension stream' T_l initialized at output layer T_L = -∇_y L (negative loss gradient direction). Propagate BACKWARD through layers via T_{l-1} = α T_l + (1-α) f_l(h_l), α=0.95 (strong cohesion). Concatenate T_l with each layer's input. Provides forward-pass-time demand signal from output to input. Differs from (a) standard residual (identity not weighted), (b) RNN backward states (sequential), (c) U-Net skip (single-skip not cohesion chain), (d) Peri-LN (norm only) by combining (i) SECONDARY tension stream + (ii) WEIGHTED α-COHESION + (iii) OUTPUT-INITIALIZED backward-in-forward propagation.
novelty_claim: Standard residual is identity. RNN backward is sequential. U-Net skip is single. Peri-LN is norm. XYLEM-TENSION recombines (a) SECONDARY tension stream + (b) α-WEIGHTED cohesion blend + (c) OUTPUT-INITIALIZED backward in forward pass. Three known components combined; backward-tension-in-forward-pass is the new combination angle.
